verses.kjv: And every soul that eateth that which died of itself, or that which was torn with beasts, whether it be one of your own country, or a stranger, he shall both wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even: then shall he be clean.
verses.bsb: And any person, whether native or foreigner, who eats anything found dead or mauled by wild beasts must wash his clothes and bathe with water, and he will be unclean until evening; then he will be clean.

verses.esv: But if he does not wash them or bathe his flesh, he shall bear his iniquity.”
verses.kjv: But if he wash them not, nor bathe his flesh; then he shall bear his iniquity.
verses.bsb: But if he does not wash his clothes and bathe himself, then he shall bear his iniquity.”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to the people of Israel and say to them, I am the LORD your God.
verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: I am the LORD your God.

verses.esv: You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt, where you lived, and you shall not do as they do in the land of Canaan, to which I am bringing you. You shall not walk in their statutes.
verses.kjv: After the doings of the land of Egypt, wherein ye dwelt, shall ye not do: and after the doings of the land of Canaan, whither I bring you, shall ye not do: neither shall ye walk in their ordinances.
verses.bsb: You must not follow the practices of the land of Egypt, where you used to live, and you must not follow the practices of the land of Canaan, into which I am bringing you. You must not walk in their customs.

verses.esv: You shall follow my rules and keep my statutes and walk in them. I am the LORD your God.
verses.kjv: Ye shall do my judgments, and keep mine ordinances, to walk therein: I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: You are to practice My judgments and keep My statutes by walking in them. I am the LORD your God.

verses.esv: You shall therefore keep my statutes and my rules; if a person does them, he shall live by them: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: Ye shall therefore keep my statutes, and my judgments: which if a man do, he shall live in them: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: Keep My statutes and My judgments, for the man who does these things will live by them. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: “None of you shall approach any one of his close relatives to uncover nakedness. I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: ¶ None of you shall approach to any that is near of kin to him, to uncover their nakedness: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: None of you are to approach any close relative to have sexual relations. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father, which is the nakedness of your mother; she is your mother, you shall not uncover her nakedness.
verses.kjv: The nakedness of thy father, or the nakedness of thy mother, shalt thou not uncover: she is thy mother; thou shalt not uncover her nakedness.
verses.bsb: You must not expose the nakedness of your father by having sexual relations with your mother. She is your mother; you must not have sexual relations with her.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's wife; it is your father's nakedness.
verses.kjv: The nakedness of thy father’s wife shalt thou not uncover: it is thy father’s nakedness.
verses.bsb: You must not have sexual relations with your father’s wife; it would dishonor your father.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your sister, your father's daughter or your mother's daughter, whether brought up in the family or in another home.
verses.kjv: The nakedness of thy sister, the daughter of thy father, or daughter of thy mother, whether she be born at home, or born abroad, even their nakedness thou shalt not uncover.
verses.bsb: You must not have sexual relations with your sister, either your father’s daughter or your mother’s daughter, whether she was born in the same home or elsewhere.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your son's daughter or of your daughter's daughter, for their nakedness is your own nakedness.
verses.kjv: The nakedness of thy son’s daughter, or of thy daughter’s daughter, even their nakedness thou shalt not uncover: for theirs is thine own nakedness.
verses.bsb: You must not have sexual relations with your son’s daughter or your daughter’s daughter, for that would shame your family.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's wife's daughter, brought up in your father's family, since she is your sister.
verses.kjv: The nakedness of thy father’s wife’s daughter, begotten of thy father, she is thy sister, thou shalt not uncover her nakedness.
verses.bsb: You must not have sexual relations with the daughter of your father’s wife, born to your father; she is your sister.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's sister; she is your father's relative.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father’s sister: she is thy father’s near kinswoman.
verses.bsb: You must not have sexual relations with your father’s sister; she is your father’s close relative.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother's sister, for she is your mother's relative.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy mother’s sister: for she is thy mother’s near kinswoman.
verses.bsb: You must not have sexual relations with your mother’s sister, for she is your mother’s close relative.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's brother, that is, you shall not approach his wife; she is your aunt.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father’s brother, thou shalt not approach to his wife: she is thine aunt.
verses.bsb: You must not dishonor your father’s brother by approaching his wife to have sexual relations with her; she is your aunt.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter-in-law; she is your son's wife, you shall not uncover her nakedness.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy daughter in law: she is thy son’s wife; thou shalt not uncover her nakedness.
verses.bsb: You must not have sexual relations with your daughter-in-law. She is your son’s wife; you are not to have sexual relations with her.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your brother's wife; it is your brother's nakedness.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy brother’s wife: it is thy brother’s nakedness.
verses.bsb: You must not have sexual relations with your brother’s wife; that would shame your brother.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of a woman and of her daughter, and you shall not take her son's daughter or her daughter's daughter to uncover her nakedness; they are relatives; it is depravity.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of a woman and her daughter, neither shalt thou take her son’s daughter, or her daughter’s daughter, to uncover her nakedness; for they are her near kinswomen: it is wickedness.
verses.bsb: You must not have sexual relations with both a woman and her daughter. You are not to marry her son’s daughter or her daughter’s daughter and have sexual relations with her. They are close relatives; it is depraved.

verses.esv: And you shall not take a woman as a rival wife to her sister, uncovering her nakedness while her sister is still alive.
verses.kjv: Neither shalt thou take a wife to her sister, to vex her, to uncover her nakedness, beside the other in her life time.
verses.bsb: You must not take your wife’s sister as a rival wife and have sexual relations with her while your wife is still alive.

verses.esv: “You shall not approach a woman to uncover her nakedness while she is in her menstrual uncleanness.
verses.kjv: Also thou shalt not approach unto a woman to uncover her nakedness, as long as she is put apart for her uncleanness.
verses.bsb: You must not approach a woman to have sexual relations with her during her menstrual period.

verses.esv: And you shall not lie sexually with your neighbor's wife and so make yourself unclean with her.
verses.kjv: Moreover thou shalt not lie carnally with thy neighbour’s wife, to defile thyself with her.
verses.bsb: You must not lie carnally with your neighbor’s wife and thus defile yourself with her.

verses.esv: You shall not give any of your children to offer them to Molech, and so profane the name of your God: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt not let any of thy seed pass through the fire to Molech, neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: You must not give any of your children to be sacrificed to Molech, for you must not profane the name of your God. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: You shall not lie with a male as with a woman; it is an abomination.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt not lie with mankind, as with womankind: it is abomination.
verses.bsb: You must not lie with a man as with a woman; that is an abomination.

verses.esv: And you shall not lie with any animal and so make yourself unclean with it, neither shall any woman give herself to an animal to lie with it: it is perversion.
verses.kjv: Neither shalt thou lie with any beast to defile thyself therewith: neither shall any woman stand before a beast to lie down thereto: it is confusion.
verses.bsb: You must not lie carnally with any animal, thus defiling yourself with it; a woman must not stand before an animal to mate with it; that is a perversion.

verses.esv: “Do not make yourselves unclean by any of these things, for by all these the nations I am driving out before you have become unclean,
verses.kjv: Defile not ye yourselves in any of these things: for in all these the nations are defiled which I cast out before you:
verses.bsb: Do not defile yourselves by any of these practices, for by all these things the nations I am driving out before you have defiled themselves.

verses.esv: and the land became unclean, so that I punished its iniquity, and the land vomited out its inhabitants.
verses.kjv: And the land is defiled: therefore I do visit the iniquity thereof upon it, and the land itself vomiteth out her inhabitants.
verses.bsb: Even the land has become defiled, so I am punishing it for its sin, and the land will vomit out its inhabitants.

verses.esv: But you shall keep my statutes and my rules and do none of these abominations, either the native or the stranger who sojourns among you
verses.kjv: Ye shall therefore keep my statutes and my judgments, and shall not commit any of these abominations; neither any of your own nation, nor any stranger that sojourneth among you:
verses.bsb: But you are to keep My statutes and ordinances, and you must not commit any of these abominations—neither your native-born nor the foreigner who lives among you.

verses.esv: (for the people of the land, who were before you, did all of these abominations, so that the land became unclean),
verses.kjv: (For all these abominations have the men of the land done, which were before you, and the land is defiled;)
verses.bsb: For the men who were in the land before you committed all these abominations, and the land has become defiled.

verses.esv: lest the land vomit you out when you make it unclean, as it vomited out the nation that was before you.
verses.kjv: That the land spue not you out also, when ye defile it, as it spued out the nations that were before you.
verses.bsb: So if you defile the land, it will vomit you out as it spewed out the nations before you.

verses.esv: For everyone who does any of these abominations, the persons who do them shall be cut off from among their people.
verses.kjv: For whosoever shall commit any of these abominations, even the souls that commit them shall be cut off from among their people.
verses.bsb: Therefore anyone who commits any of these abominations must be cut off from among his people.

verses.esv: So keep my charge never to practice any of these abominable customs that were practiced before you, and never to make yourselves unclean by them: I am the LORD your God.”
verses.kjv: Therefore shall ye keep mine ordinance, that ye commit not any one of these abominable customs, which were committed before you, and that ye defile not yourselves therein: I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: You must keep My charge not to practice any of the abominable customs that were practiced before you, so that you do not defile yourselves by them. I am the LORD your God.”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to all the congregation of the people of Israel and say to them, You shall be holy, for I the LORD your God am holy.
verses.kjv: Speak unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, and say unto them, Ye shall be holy: for I the Lord your God am holy.
verses.bsb: “Speak to the whole congregation of Israel and tell them: Be holy because I, the LORD your God, am holy.

verses.esv: Every one of you shall revere his mother and his father, and you shall keep my Sabbaths: I am the LORD your God.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye shall fear every man his mother, and his father, and keep my sabbaths: I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: Each of you must respect his mother and father, and you must keep My Sabbaths. I am the LORD your God.

verses.esv: Do not turn to idols or make for yourselves any gods of cast metal: I am the LORD your God.
verses.kjv: ¶ Turn ye not unto idols, nor make to yourselves molten gods: I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: Do not turn to idols or make for yourselves molten gods. I am the LORD your God.

verses.esv: “When you offer a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD, you shall offer it so that you may be accepted.
verses.kjv: ¶ And if ye offer a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the Lord, ye shall offer it at your own will.
verses.bsb: When you sacrifice a peace offering to the LORD, you shall offer it for your acceptance.

verses.esv: It shall be eaten the same day you offer it or on the day after, and anything left over until the third day shall be burned up with fire.
verses.kjv: It shall be eaten the same day ye offer it, and on the morrow: and if ought remain until the third day, it shall be burnt in the fire.
verses.bsb: It shall be eaten on the day you sacrifice it, or on the next day; but what remains on the third day must be burned up.

verses.esv: If it is eaten at all on the third day, it is tainted; it will not be accepted,
verses.kjv: And if it be eaten at all on the third day, it is abominable; it shall not be accepted.
verses.bsb: If any of it is eaten on the third day, it is tainted and will not be accepted.

verses.esv: and everyone who eats it shall bear his iniquity, because he has profaned what is holy to the LORD, and that person shall be cut off from his people.
verses.kjv: Therefore every one that eateth it shall bear his iniquity, because he hath profaned the hallowed thing of the Lord: and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.
verses.bsb: Whoever eats it will bear his iniquity, for he has profaned what is holy to the LORD. That person must be cut off from his people.

verses.esv: “When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap your field right up to its edge, neither shall you gather the gleanings after your harvest.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not wholly reap the corners of thy field, neither shalt thou gather the gleanings of thy harvest.
verses.bsb: When you reap the harvest of your land, you are not to reap to the very edges of your field or gather the gleanings of your harvest.

verses.esv: And you shall not strip your vineyard bare, neither shall you gather the fallen grapes of your vineyard. You shall leave them for the poor and for the sojourner: I am the LORD your God.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt not glean thy vineyard, neither shalt thou gather every grape of thy vineyard; thou shalt leave them for the poor and stranger: I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: You must not strip your vineyard bare or gather its fallen grapes. Leave them for the poor and the foreigner. I am the LORD your God.

verses.esv: “You shall not steal; you shall not deal falsely; you shall not lie to one another.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye shall not steal, neither deal falsely, neither lie one to another.
verses.bsb: You must not steal. You must not lie or deceive one another.

verses.esv: You shall not swear by my name falsely, and so profane the name of your God: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: ¶ And ye shall not swear by my name falsely, neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: You must not swear falsely by My name and so profane the name of your God. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: “You shall not oppress your neighbor or rob him. The wages of a hired worker shall not remain with you all night until the morning.
verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt not defraud thy neighbour, neither rob him: the wages of him that is hired shall not abide with thee all night until the morning.
verses.bsb: You must not defraud your neighbor or rob him.  You must not withhold until morning the wages due a hired hand.

verses.esv: You shall not curse the deaf or put a stumbling block before the blind, but you shall fear your God: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt not curse the deaf, nor put a stumblingblock before the blind, but shalt fear thy God: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: You must not curse the deaf or place a stumbling block before the blind, but you shall fear your God. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: “You shall do no injustice in court. You shall not be partial to the poor or defer to the great, but in righteousness shall you judge your neighbor.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye shall do no unrighteousness in judgment: thou shalt not respect the person of the poor, nor honour the person of the mighty: but in righteousness shalt thou judge thy neighbour.
verses.bsb: You must not pervert justice; you must not show partiality to the poor or favoritism to the rich; you are to judge your neighbor fairly.

verses.esv: You shall not go around as a slanderer among your people, and you shall not stand up against the life of your neighbor: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt not go up and down as a talebearer among thy people: neither shalt thou stand against the blood of thy neighbour: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: You must not go about spreading slander among your people.  You must not endanger the life of your neighbor. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: “You shall not hate your brother in your heart, but you shall reason frankly with your neighbor, lest you incur sin because of him.
verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thine heart: thou shalt in any wise rebuke thy neighbour, and not suffer sin upon him.
verses.bsb: You must not harbor hatred against your brother in your heart. Directly rebuke your neighbor, so that you will not incur guilt on account of him.

verses.esv: You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against the sons of your own people, but you shall love your neighbor as yourself: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt not avenge, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: Do not seek revenge or bear a grudge against any of your people, but love your neighbor as yourself. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: “You shall keep my statutes. You shall not let your cattle breed with a different kind. You shall not sow your field with two kinds of seed, nor shall you wear a garment of cloth made of two kinds of material.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye shall keep my statutes. Thou shalt not let thy cattle gender with a diverse kind: thou shalt not sow thy field with mingled seed: neither shall a garment mingled of linen and woollen come upon thee.
verses.bsb: You are to keep My statutes. You shall not crossbreed two different kinds of livestock; you shall not sow your fields with two kinds of seed; and you shall not wear clothing made of two kinds of material.

verses.esv: “If a man lies sexually with a woman who is a slave, assigned to another man and not yet ransomed or given her freedom, a distinction shall be made. They shall not be put to death, because she was not free;
verses.kjv: ¶ And whosoever lieth carnally with a woman, that is a bondmaid, betrothed to an husband, and not at all redeemed, nor freedom given her; she shall be scourged; they shall not be put to death, because she was not free.
verses.bsb: If a man lies carnally with a slave girl promised to another man but who has not been redeemed or given her freedom, there must be due punishment. But they are not to be put to death, because she had not been freed.

verses.esv: but he shall bring his compensation to the LORD, to the entrance of the tent of meeting, a ram for a guilt offering.
verses.kjv: And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the Lord, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, even a ram for a trespass offering.
verses.bsb: The man, however, must bring a ram to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting as his guilt offering to the LORD.

verses.esv: And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering before the LORD for his sin that he has committed, and he shall be forgiven for the sin that he has committed.
verses.kjv: And the priest shall make an atonement for him with the ram of the trespass offering before the Lord for his sin which he hath done: and the sin which he hath done shall be forgiven him.
verses.bsb: The priest shall make atonement on his behalf before the LORD with the ram of the guilt offering for the sin he has committed, and he will be forgiven the sin he has committed.

verses.esv: “When you come into the land and plant any kind of tree for food, then you shall regard its fruit as forbidden. Three years it shall be forbidden to you; it must not be eaten.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when ye shall come into the land, and shall have planted all manner of trees for food, then ye shall count the fruit thereof as uncircumcised: three years shall it be as uncircumcised unto you: it shall not be eaten of.
verses.bsb: When you enter the land and plant any kind of tree for food, you shall regard the fruit as forbidden. For three years it will be forbidden to you and must not be eaten.

verses.esv: And in the fourth year all its fruit shall be holy, an offering of praise to the LORD.
verses.kjv: But in the fourth year all the fruit thereof shall be holy to praise the Lord withal.
verses.bsb: In the fourth year all its fruit must be consecrated as a praise offering to the LORD.

verses.esv: But in the fifth year you may eat of its fruit, to increase its yield for you: I am the LORD your God.
verses.kjv: And in the fifth year shall ye eat of the fruit thereof, that it may yield unto you the increase thereof: I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: But in the fifth year you may eat its fruit; thus your harvest will be increased. I am the LORD your God.

verses.esv: “You shall not eat any flesh with the blood in it. You shall not interpret omens or tell fortunes.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye shall not eat any thing with the blood: neither shall ye use enchantment, nor observe times.
verses.bsb: You must not eat anything with blood still in it.  You must not practice divination or sorcery.

verses.esv: You shall not round off the hair on your temples or mar the edges of your beard.
verses.kjv: Ye shall not round the corners of your heads, neither shalt thou mar the corners of thy beard.
verses.bsb: You must not cut off the hair at the sides of your head or clip off the edges of your beard.

verses.esv: You shall not make any cuts on your body for the dead or tattoo yourselves: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: Ye shall not make any cuttings in your flesh for the dead, nor print any marks upon you: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: You must not make any cuts in your bodies for the dead or put tattoo marks on yourselves. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: “Do not profane your daughter by making her a prostitute, lest the land fall into prostitution and the land become full of depravity.
verses.kjv: ¶ Do not prostitute thy daughter, to cause her to be a whore; lest the land fall to whoredom, and the land become full of wickedness.
verses.bsb: You must not defile your daughter by making her a prostitute, or the land will be prostituted and filled with depravity.

verses.esv: You shall keep my Sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye shall keep my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: You must keep My Sabbaths and have reverence for My sanctuary. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: “Do not turn to mediums or necromancers; do not seek them out, and so make yourselves unclean by them: I am the LORD your God.
verses.kjv: ¶ Regard not them that have familiar spirits, neither seek after wizards, to be defiled by them: I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: You must not turn to mediums or spiritists; do not seek them out, or you will be defiled by them. I am the LORD your God.

verses.esv: “You shall stand up before the gray head and honor the face of an old man, and you shall fear your God: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: ¶ Thou shalt rise up before the hoary head, and honour the face of the old man, and fear thy God: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: You are to rise in the presence of the elderly, honor the aged, and fear your God. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: “When a stranger sojourns with you in your land, you shall not do him wrong.
verses.kjv: ¶ And if a stranger sojourn with thee in your land, ye shall not vex him.
verses.bsb: When a foreigner resides with you in your land, you must not oppress him.

verses.esv: You shall treat the stranger who sojourns with you as the native among you, and you shall love him as yourself, for you were strangers in the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.
verses.kjv: But the stranger that dwelleth with you shall be unto you as one born among you, and thou shalt love him as thyself; for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: You must treat the foreigner living among you as native-born and love him as yourself, for you were foreigners in the land of Egypt. I am the LORD your God.

verses.esv: “You shall do no wrong in judgment, in measures of length or weight or quantity.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye shall do no unrighteousness in judgment, in meteyard, in weight, or in measure.
verses.bsb: You must not use dishonest measures of length, weight, or volume.

verses.esv: You shall have just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.
verses.kjv: Just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin, shall ye have: I am the Lord your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: You shall maintain honest scales and weights, an honest ephah, and an honest hin. I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.

verses.esv: And you shall observe all my statutes and all my rules, and do them: I am the LORD.”
verses.kjv: Therefore shall ye observe all my statutes, and all my judgments, and do them: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: You must keep all My statutes and all My ordinances and follow them. I am the LORD.”

verses.esv: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Say to the people of Israel, Any one of the people of Israel or of the strangers who sojourn in Israel who gives any of his children to Molech shall surely be put to death. The people of the land shall stone him with stones.
verses.kjv: Again, thou shalt say to the children of Israel, Whosoever he be of the children of Israel, or of the strangers that sojourn in Israel, that giveth any of his seed unto Molech; he shall surely be put to death: the people of the land shall stone him with stones.
verses.bsb: “Tell the Israelites, ‘Any Israelite or foreigner living in Israel who gives any of his children to Molech must be put to death. The people of the land are to stone him.

verses.esv: I myself will set my face against that man and will cut him off from among his people, because he has given one of his children to Molech, to make my sanctuary unclean and to profane my holy name.
verses.kjv: And I will set my face against that man, and will cut him off from among his people; because he hath given of his seed unto Molech, to defile my sanctuary, and to profane my holy name.
verses.bsb: And I will set My face against that man and cut him off from his people, because by giving his offspring to Molech, he has defiled My sanctuary and profaned My holy name.

verses.esv: And if the people of the land do at all close their eyes to that man when he gives one of his children to Molech, and do not put him to death,
verses.kjv: And if the people of the land do any ways hide their eyes from the man, when he giveth of his seed unto Molech, and kill him not:
verses.bsb: And if the people of the land ever hide their eyes and fail to put to death the man who gives one of his children to Molech,

verses.esv: then I will set my face against that man and against his clan and will cut them off from among their people, him and all who follow him in whoring after Molech.
verses.kjv: Then I will set my face against that man, and against his family, and will cut him off, and all that go a whoring after him, to commit whoredom with Molech, from among their people.
verses.bsb: then I will set My face against that man and his family and cut off from among their people both him and all who follow him in prostituting themselves with Molech.

verses.esv: “If a person turns to mediums and necromancers, whoring after them, I will set my face against that person and will cut him off from among his people.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the soul that turneth after such as have familiar spirits, and after wizards, to go a whoring after them, I will even set my face against that soul, and will cut him off from among his people.
verses.bsb: Whoever turns to mediums or spiritists to prostitute himself with them, I will also set My face against that person and cut him off from his people.

verses.esv: Consecrate yourselves, therefore, and be holy, for I am the LORD your God.
verses.kjv: ¶ Sanctify yourselves therefore, and be ye holy: for I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: Consecrate yourselves, therefore, and be holy, because I am the LORD your God.

verses.esv: Keep my statutes and do them; I am the LORD who sanctifies you.
verses.kjv: And ye shall keep my statutes, and do them: I am the Lord which sanctify you.
verses.bsb: And you shall keep My statutes and practice them. I am the LORD who sanctifies you.

verses.esv: For anyone who curses his father or his mother shall surely be put to death; he has cursed his father or his mother; his blood is upon him.
verses.kjv: ¶ For every one that curseth his father or his mother shall be surely put to death: he hath cursed his father or his mother; his blood shall be upon him.
verses.bsb: If anyone curses his father or mother, he must be put to death. He has cursed his father or mother; his blood shall be upon him.

verses.esv: “If a man commits adultery with the wife of his neighbor, both the adulterer and the adulteress shall surely be put to death.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the man that committeth adultery with another man’s wife, even he that committeth adultery with his neighbour’s wife, the adulterer and the adulteress shall surely be put to death.
verses.bsb: If a man commits adultery with another man’s wife—with the wife of his neighbor—both the adulterer and the adulteress must surely be put to death.

verses.esv: If a man lies with his father's wife, he has uncovered his father's nakedness; both of them shall surely be put to death; their blood is upon them.
verses.kjv: And the man that lieth with his father’s wife hath uncovered his father’s nakedness: both of them shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.
verses.bsb: If a man lies with his father’s wife, he has uncovered his father’s nakedness. Both must surely be put to death; their blood is upon them.

verses.esv: If a man lies with his daughter-in-law, both of them shall surely be put to death; they have committed perversion; their blood is upon them.
verses.kjv: And if a man lie with his daughter in law, both of them shall surely be put to death: they have wrought confusion; their blood shall be upon them.
verses.bsb: If a man lies with his daughter-in-law, both must surely be put to death. They have acted perversely; their blood is upon them.

verses.esv: If a man lies with a male as with a woman, both of them have committed an abomination; they shall surely be put to death; their blood is upon them.
verses.kjv: If a man also lie with mankind, as he lieth with a woman, both of them have committed an abomination: they shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.
verses.bsb: If a man lies with a man as with a woman, they have both committed an abomination. They must surely be put to death; their blood is upon them.

verses.esv: If a man takes a woman and her mother also, it is depravity; he and they shall be burned with fire, that there may be no depravity among you.
verses.kjv: And if a man take a wife and her mother, it is wickedness: they shall be burnt with fire, both he and they; that there be no wickedness among you.
verses.bsb: If a man marries both a woman and her mother, it is depraved. Both he and they must be burned in the fire, so that there will be no depravity among you.

verses.esv: If a man lies with an animal, he shall surely be put to death, and you shall kill the animal.
verses.kjv: And if a man lie with a beast, he shall surely be put to death: and ye shall slay the beast.
verses.bsb: If a man lies carnally with an animal, he must be put to death. And you are also to kill the animal.

verses.esv: If a woman approaches any animal and lies with it, you shall kill the woman and the animal; they shall surely be put to death; their blood is upon them.
verses.kjv: And if a woman approach unto any beast, and lie down thereto, thou shalt kill the woman, and the beast: they shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.
verses.bsb: If a woman approaches any animal to mate with it, you must kill both the woman and the animal. They must surely be put to death; their blood is upon them.

verses.esv: “If a man takes his sister, a daughter of his father or a daughter of his mother, and sees her nakedness, and she sees his nakedness, it is a disgrace, and they shall be cut off in the sight of the children of their people. He has uncovered his sister's nakedness, and he shall bear his iniquity.
verses.kjv: And if a man shall take his sister, his father’s daughter, or his mother’s daughter, and see her nakedness, and she see his nakedness; it is a wicked thing; and they shall be cut off in the sight of their people: he hath uncovered his sister’s nakedness; he shall bear his iniquity.
verses.bsb: If a man marries his sister, whether the daughter of his father or of his mother, and they have sexual relations, it is a disgrace. They must be cut off in the sight of their people. He has uncovered the nakedness of his sister; he shall bear his iniquity.

verses.esv: If a man lies with a woman during her menstrual period and uncovers her nakedness, he has made naked her fountain, and she has uncovered the fountain of her blood. Both of them shall be cut off from among their people.
verses.kjv: And if a man shall lie with a woman having her sickness, and shall uncover her nakedness; he hath discovered her fountain, and she hath uncovered the fountain of her blood: and both of them shall be cut off from among their people.
verses.bsb: If a man lies with a menstruating woman and has sexual relations with her, he has exposed the source of her flow, and she has uncovered the source of her blood. Both of them must be cut off from among their people.

verses.esv: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother's sister or of your father's sister, for that is to make naked one's relative; they shall bear their iniquity.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy mother’s sister, nor of thy father’s sister: for he uncovereth his near kin: they shall bear their iniquity.
verses.bsb: You must not have sexual relations with the sister of your mother or your father, for it is exposing one’s own kin; both shall bear their iniquity.

verses.esv: If a man lies with his uncle's wife, he has uncovered his uncle's nakedness; they shall bear their sin; they shall die childless.
verses.kjv: And if a man shall lie with his uncle’s wife, he hath uncovered his uncle’s nakedness: they shall bear their sin; they shall die childless.
verses.bsb: If a man lies with his uncle’s wife, he has uncovered the nakedness of his uncle. They will bear their sin; they shall die childless.

verses.esv: If a man takes his brother's wife, it is impurity. He has uncovered his brother's nakedness; they shall be childless.
verses.kjv: And if a man shall take his brother’s wife, it is an unclean thing: he hath uncovered his brother’s nakedness; they shall be childless.
verses.bsb: If a man marries his brother’s wife, it is an act of impurity. He has uncovered the nakedness of his brother; they shall be childless.

verses.esv: “You shall therefore keep all my statutes and all my rules and do them, that the land where I am bringing you to live may not vomit you out.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye shall therefore keep all my statutes, and all my judgments, and do them: that the land, whither I bring you to dwell therein, spue you not out.
verses.bsb: You are therefore to keep all My statutes and ordinances, so that the land where I am bringing you to live will not vomit you out.

verses.esv: And you shall not walk in the customs of the nation that I am driving out before you, for they did all these things, and therefore I detested them.
verses.kjv: And ye shall not walk in the manners of the nation, which I cast out before you: for they committed all these things, and therefore I abhorred them.
verses.bsb: You must not follow the statutes of the nations I am driving out before you. Because they did all these things, I abhorred them.

verses.esv: But I have said to you, ‘You shall inherit their land, and I will give it to you to possess, a land flowing with milk and honey.’ I am the LORD your God, who has separated you from the peoples.
verses.kjv: But I have said unto you, Ye shall inherit their land, and I will give it unto you to possess it, a land that floweth with milk and honey: I am the Lord your God, which have separated you from other people.
verses.bsb: But I have told you that you will inherit their land, since I will give it to you as an inheritance—a land flowing with milk and honey. I am the LORD your God, who has set you apart from the peoples.

verses.esv: You shall therefore separate the clean beast from the unclean, and the unclean bird from the clean. You shall not make yourselves detestable by beast or by bird or by anything with which the ground crawls, which I have set apart for you to hold unclean.
verses.kjv: Ye shall therefore put difference between clean beasts and unclean, and between unclean fowls and clean: and ye shall not make your souls abominable by beast, or by fowl, or by any manner of living thing that creepeth on the ground, which I have separated from you as unclean.
verses.bsb: You are therefore to distinguish between clean and unclean animals and birds. Do not become contaminated by any animal or bird, or by anything that crawls on the ground; I have set these apart as unclean for you.

verses.esv: You shall be holy to me, for I the LORD am holy and have separated you from the peoples, that you should be mine.
verses.kjv: And ye shall be holy unto me: for I the Lord am holy, and have severed you from other people, that ye should be mine.
verses.bsb: You are to be holy to Me because I, the LORD, am holy, and I have set you apart from the nations to be My own.

verses.esv: “A man or a woman who is a medium or a necromancer shall surely be put to death. They shall be stoned with stones; their blood shall be upon them.”
verses.kjv: ¶ A man also or woman that hath a familiar spirit, or that is a wizard, shall surely be put to death: they shall stone them with stones: their blood shall be upon them.
verses.bsb: A man or a woman who is a medium or spiritist must surely be put to death. They shall be stoned; their blood is upon them.’”

verses.esv: And the LORD said to Moses, “Speak to the priests, the sons of Aaron, and say to them, No one shall make himself unclean for the dead among his people,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord said unto Moses, Speak unto the priests the sons of Aaron, and say unto them, There shall none be defiled for the dead among his people:
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Speak to Aaron’s sons, the priests, and tell them that a priest is not to defile himself for a dead person among his people,

verses.esv: except for his closest relatives, his mother, his father, his son, his daughter, his brother,
verses.kjv: But for his kin, that is near unto him, that is, for his mother, and for his father, and for his son, and for his daughter, and for his brother,
verses.bsb: except for his immediate family—his mother, father, son, daughter, or brother,

verses.esv: or his virgin sister (who is near to him because she has had no husband; for her he may make himself unclean).
verses.kjv: And for his sister a virgin, that is nigh unto him, which hath had no husband; for her may he be defiled.
verses.bsb: or his unmarried sister who is near to him, since she has no husband.

verses.esv: He shall not make himself unclean as a husband among his people and so profane himself.
verses.kjv: But he shall not defile himself, being a chief man among his people, to profane himself.
verses.bsb: He is not to defile himself for those related to him by marriage, and so profane himself.

verses.esv: They shall not make bald patches on their heads, nor shave off the edges of their beards, nor make any cuts on their body.
verses.kjv: They shall not make baldness upon their head, neither shall they shave off the corner of their beard, nor make any cuttings in their flesh.
verses.bsb: Priests must not make bald spots on their heads, shave off the edges of their beards, or make cuts in their bodies.

verses.esv: They shall be holy to their God and not profane the name of their God. For they offer the LORD's food offerings, the bread of their God; therefore they shall be holy.
verses.kjv: They shall be holy unto their God, and not profane the name of their God: for the offerings of the Lord made by fire, and the bread of their God, they do offer: therefore they shall be holy.
verses.bsb: They must be holy to their God and not profane the name of their God. Because they present to the LORD the offerings made by fire, the food of their God, they must be holy.

verses.esv: They shall not marry a prostitute or a woman who has been defiled, neither shall they marry a woman divorced from her husband, for the priest is holy to his God.
verses.kjv: They shall not take a wife that is a whore, or profane; neither shall they take a woman put away from her husband: for he is holy unto his God.
verses.bsb: A priest must not marry a woman defiled by prostitution or divorced by her husband, for the priest is holy to his God.

verses.esv: You shall sanctify him, for he offers the bread of your God. He shall be holy to you, for I, the LORD, who sanctify you, am holy.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt sanctify him therefore; for he offereth the bread of thy God: he shall be holy unto thee: for I the Lord, which sanctify you, am holy.
verses.bsb: You are to regard him as holy, since he presents the food of your God. He shall be holy to you, because I the LORD am holy—I who set you apart.

verses.esv: And the daughter of any priest, if she profanes herself by whoring, profanes her father; she shall be burned with fire.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the daughter of any priest, if she profane herself by playing the whore, she profaneth her father: she shall be burnt with fire.
verses.bsb: If a priest’s daughter defiles herself by prostituting herself, she profanes her father; she must be burned in the fire.

verses.esv: “The priest who is chief among his brothers, on whose head the anointing oil is poured and who has been consecrated to wear the garments, shall not let the hair of his head hang loose nor tear his clothes.
verses.kjv: And he that is the high priest among his brethren, upon whose head the anointing oil was poured, and that is consecrated to put on the garments, shall not uncover his head, nor rend his clothes;
verses.bsb: The priest who is highest among his brothers, who has had the anointing oil poured on his head and has been ordained to wear the priestly garments, must not let his hair hang loose or tear his garments.

verses.esv: He shall not go in to any dead bodies nor make himself unclean, even for his father or for his mother.
verses.kjv: Neither shall he go in to any dead body, nor defile himself for his father, or for his mother;
verses.bsb: He must not go near any dead body; he must not defile himself, even for his father or mother.

verses.esv: He shall not go out of the sanctuary, lest he profane the sanctuary of his God, for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is on him: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: Neither shall he go out of the sanctuary, nor profane the sanctuary of his God; for the crown of the anointing oil of his God is upon him: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: He must not leave or desecrate the sanctuary of his God, for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is on him. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: And he shall take a wife in her virginity.
verses.kjv: And he shall take a wife in her virginity.
verses.bsb: The woman he marries must be a virgin.

verses.esv: A widow, or a divorced woman, or a woman who has been defiled, or a prostitute, these he shall not marry. But he shall take as his wife a virgin of his own people,
verses.kjv: A widow, or a divorced woman, or profane, or an harlot, these shall he not take: but he shall take a virgin of his own people to wife.
verses.bsb: He is not to marry a widow, a divorced woman, or one defiled by prostitution. He is to marry a virgin from his own people,

verses.esv: that he may not profane his offspring among his people, for I am the LORD who sanctifies him.”
verses.kjv: Neither shall he profane his seed among his people: for I the Lord do sanctify him.
verses.bsb: so that he does not defile his offspring among his people, for I am the LORD who sanctifies him.”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to Aaron, saying, None of your offspring throughout their generations who has a blemish may approach to offer the bread of his God.
verses.kjv: Speak unto Aaron, saying, Whosoever he be of thy seed in their generations that hath any blemish, let him not approach to offer the bread of his God.
verses.bsb: “Say to Aaron, ‘For the generations to come, none of your descendants who has a physical defect may approach to offer the food of his God.

verses.esv: For no one who has a blemish shall draw near, a man blind or lame, or one who has a mutilated face or a limb too long,
verses.kjv: For whatsoever man he be that hath a blemish, he shall not approach: a blind man, or a lame, or he that hath a flat nose, or any thing superfluous,
verses.bsb: No man who has any defect may approach—no man who is blind, lame, disfigured, or deformed;

verses.esv: or a man who has an injured foot or an injured hand,
verses.kjv: Or a man that is brokenfooted, or brokenhanded,
verses.bsb: no man who has a broken foot or hand,

verses.esv: or a hunchback or a dwarf or a man with a defect in his sight or an itching disease or scabs or crushed testicles.
verses.kjv: Or crookbackt, or a dwarf, or that hath a blemish in his eye, or be scurvy, or scabbed, or hath his stones broken;
verses.bsb: or who is a hunchback or dwarf, or who has an eye defect, a festering rash, scabs, or a crushed testicle.

verses.esv: No man of the offspring of Aaron the priest who has a blemish shall come near to offer the LORD's food offerings; since he has a blemish, he shall not come near to offer the bread of his God.
verses.kjv: No man that hath a blemish of the seed of Aaron the priest shall come nigh to offer the offerings of the Lord made by fire: he hath a blemish; he shall not come nigh to offer the bread of his God.
verses.bsb: No descendant of Aaron the priest who has a defect shall approach to present the offerings made by fire to the LORD. Since he has a defect, he is not to come near to offer the food of his God.

verses.esv: He may eat the bread of his God, both of the most holy and of the holy things,
verses.kjv: He shall eat the bread of his God, both of the most holy, and of the holy.
verses.bsb: He may eat the most holy food of his God as well as the holy food,

verses.esv: but he shall not go through the veil or approach the altar, because he has a blemish, that he may not profane my sanctuaries, for I am the LORD who sanctifies them.”
verses.kjv: Only he shall not go in unto the vail, nor come nigh unto the altar, because he hath a blemish; that he profane not my sanctuaries: for I the Lord do sanctify them.
verses.bsb: but because he has a defect, he must not go near the veil or approach the altar, so as not to desecrate My sanctuaries. For I am the LORD who sanctifies them.’”

verses.esv: So Moses spoke to Aaron and to his sons and to all the people of Israel.
verses.kjv: And Moses told it unto Aaron, and to his sons, and unto all the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: Moses told this to Aaron and his sons and to all the Israelites.

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to Aaron and his sons so that they abstain from the holy things of the people of Israel, which they dedicate to me, so that they do not profane my holy name: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, that they separate themselves from the holy things of the children of Israel, and that they profane not my holy name in those things which they hallow unto me: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: “Tell Aaron and his sons to treat with respect the sacred offerings that the Israelites have consecrated to Me, so that they do not profane My holy name. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: Say to them, ‘If any one of all your offspring throughout your generations approaches the holy things that the people of Israel dedicate to the LORD, while he has an uncleanness, that person shall be cut off from my presence: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: Say unto them, Whosoever he be of all your seed among your generations, that goeth unto the holy things, which the children of Israel hallow unto the Lord, having his uncleanness upon him, that soul shall be cut off from my presence: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: Tell them that for the generations to come, if any of their descendants in a state of uncleanness approaches the sacred offerings that the Israelites consecrate to the LORD, that person must be cut off from My presence. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: None of the offspring of Aaron who has a leprous disease or a discharge may eat of the holy things until he is clean. Whoever touches anything that is unclean through contact with the dead or a man who has had an emission of semen,
verses.kjv: What man soever of the seed of Aaron is a leper, or hath a running issue; he shall not eat of the holy things, until he be clean. And whoso toucheth any thing that is unclean by the dead, or a man whose seed goeth from him;
verses.bsb: If a descendant of Aaron has a skin disease or a discharge, he may not eat the sacred offerings until he is clean. Whoever touches anything defiled by a corpse or by a man who has an emission of semen,

verses.esv: and whoever touches a swarming thing by which he may be made unclean or a person from whom he may take uncleanness, whatever his uncleanness may be—
verses.kjv: Or whosoever toucheth any creeping thing, whereby he may be made unclean, or a man of whom he may take uncleanness, whatsoever uncleanness he hath;
verses.bsb: or whoever touches a crawling creature or a person that makes him unclean, whatever the uncleanness may be—

verses.esv: the person who touches such a thing shall be unclean until the evening and shall not eat of the holy things unless he has bathed his body in water.
verses.kjv: The soul which hath touched any such shall be unclean until even, and shall not eat of the holy things, unless he wash his flesh with water.
verses.bsb: the man who touches any of these will remain unclean until evening. He must not eat from the sacred offerings unless he has bathed himself with water.

verses.esv: When the sun goes down he shall be clean, and afterward he may eat of the holy things, because they are his food.
verses.kjv: And when the sun is down, he shall be clean, and shall afterward eat of the holy things; because it is his food.
verses.bsb: When the sun has set, he will become clean, and then he may eat from the sacred offerings, for they are his food.

verses.esv: He shall not eat what dies of itself or is torn by beasts, and so make himself unclean by it: I am the LORD.’
verses.kjv: That which dieth of itself, or is torn with beasts, he shall not eat to defile himself therewith: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: He must not eat anything found dead or torn by wild animals, which would make him unclean. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: They shall therefore keep my charge, lest they bear sin for it and die thereby when they profane it: I am the LORD who sanctifies them.
verses.kjv: They shall therefore keep mine ordinance, lest they bear sin for it, and die therefore, if they profane it: I the Lord do sanctify them.
verses.bsb: The priests must keep My charge, lest they bear the guilt and die because they profane it. I am the LORD who sanctifies them.

verses.esv: “A lay person shall not eat of a holy thing; no foreign guest of the priest or hired worker shall eat of a holy thing,
verses.kjv: There shall no stranger eat of the holy thing: a sojourner of the priest, or an hired servant, shall not eat of the holy thing.
verses.bsb: No one outside a priest’s family may eat the sacred offering, nor may the guest of a priest or his hired hand eat it.

verses.esv: but if a priest buys a slave as his property for money, the slave may eat of it, and anyone born in his house may eat of his food.
verses.kjv: But if the priest buy any soul with his money, he shall eat of it, and he that is born in his house: they shall eat of his meat.
verses.bsb: But if a priest buys a slave with his own money, or if a slave is born in his household, that slave may eat his food.

verses.esv: If a priest's daughter marries a layman, she shall not eat of the contribution of the holy things.
verses.kjv: If the priest’s daughter also be married unto a stranger, she may not eat of an offering of the holy things.
verses.bsb: If the priest’s daughter is married to a man other than a priest, she is not to eat of the sacred contributions.

verses.esv: But if a priest's daughter is widowed or divorced and has no child and returns to her father's house, as in her youth, she may eat of her father's food; yet no lay person shall eat of it.
verses.kjv: But if the priest’s daughter be a widow, or divorced, and have no child, and is returned unto her father’s house, as in her youth, she shall eat of her father’s meat: but there shall no stranger eat thereof.
verses.bsb: But if a priest’s daughter with no children becomes widowed or divorced and returns to her father’s house, she may share her father’s food as in her youth. But no outsider may share it.

verses.esv: And if anyone eats of a holy thing unintentionally, he shall add the fifth of its value to it and give the holy thing to the priest.
verses.kjv: ¶ And if a man eat of the holy thing unwittingly, then he shall put the fifth part thereof unto it, and shall give it unto the priest with the holy thing.
verses.bsb: If anyone eats a sacred offering in error, he must add a fifth to its value and give the sacred offering to the priest.

verses.esv: They shall not profane the holy things of the people of Israel, which they contribute to the LORD,
verses.kjv: And they shall not profane the holy things of the children of Israel, which they offer unto the Lord;
verses.bsb: The priests must not profane the sacred offerings that the Israelites present to the LORD

verses.esv: and so cause them to bear iniquity and guilt, by eating their holy things: for I am the LORD who sanctifies them.”
verses.kjv: Or suffer them to bear the iniquity of trespass, when they eat their holy things: for I the Lord do sanctify them.
verses.bsb: by allowing the people to eat the sacred offerings and thus to bear the punishment for guilt. For I am the LORD who sanctifies them.”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to Aaron and his sons and all the people of Israel and say to them, When any one of the house of Israel or of the sojourners in Israel presents a burnt offering as his offering, for any of their vows or freewill offerings that they offer to the LORD,
verses.kjv: Speak unto Aaron, and to his sons, and unto all the children of Israel, and say unto them, Whatsoever he be of the house of Israel, or of the strangers in Israel, that will offer his oblation for all his vows, and for all his freewill offerings, which they will offer unto the Lord for a burnt offering;
verses.bsb: “Speak to Aaron and his sons and all the Israelites and tell them, ‘Any man of the house of Israel or any foreign resident who presents a gift for a burnt offering to the LORD, whether to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering,

verses.esv: if it is to be accepted for you it shall be a male without blemish, of the bulls or the sheep or the goats.
verses.kjv: Ye shall offer at your own will a male without blemish, of the beeves, of the sheep, or of the goats.
verses.bsb: must offer an unblemished male from the cattle, sheep, or goats in order for it to be accepted on your behalf.

verses.esv: You shall not offer anything that has a blemish, for it will not be acceptable for you.
verses.kjv: But whatsoever hath a blemish, that shall ye not offer: for it shall not be acceptable for you.
verses.bsb: You must not present anything with a defect, because it will not be accepted on your behalf.

verses.esv: And when anyone offers a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering from the herd or from the flock, to be accepted it must be perfect; there shall be no blemish in it.
verses.kjv: And whosoever offereth a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the Lord to accomplish his vow, or a freewill offering in beeves or sheep, it shall be perfect to be accepted; there shall be no blemish therein.
verses.bsb: When a man presents a peace offering to the LORD from the herd or flock to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering, it must be without blemish or defect to be acceptable.

verses.esv: Animals blind or disabled or mutilated or having a discharge or an itch or scabs you shall not offer to the LORD or give them to the LORD as a food offering on the altar.
verses.kjv: Blind, or broken, or maimed, or having a wen, or scurvy, or scabbed, ye shall not offer these unto the Lord, nor make an offering by fire of them upon the altar unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: You are not to present to the LORD any animal that is blind, injured, or maimed, or anything with a running sore, a festering rash, or a scab; you must not put any of these on the altar as an offering made by fire to the LORD.

verses.esv: You may present a bull or a lamb that has a part too long or too short for a freewill offering, but for a vow offering it cannot be accepted.
verses.kjv: Either a bullock or a lamb that hath any thing superfluous or lacking in his parts, that mayest thou offer for a freewill offering; but for a vow it shall not be accepted.
verses.bsb: You may present as a freewill offering an ox or sheep that has a deformed or stunted limb, but it is not acceptable in fulfillment of a vow.

verses.esv: Any animal that has its testicles bruised or crushed or torn or cut you shall not offer to the LORD; you shall not do it within your land,
verses.kjv: Ye shall not offer unto the Lord that which is bruised, or crushed, or broken, or cut; neither shall ye make any offering thereof in your land.
verses.bsb: You are not to present to the LORD an animal whose testicles are bruised, crushed, torn, or cut; you are not to sacrifice them in your land.

verses.esv: neither shall you offer as the bread of your God any such animals gotten from a foreigner. Since there is a blemish in them, because of their mutilation, they will not be accepted for you.”
verses.kjv: Neither from a stranger’s hand shall ye offer the bread of your God of any of these; because their corruption is in them, and blemishes be in them: they shall not be accepted for you.
verses.bsb: Neither you nor a foreigner shall present food to your God from any such animal. They will not be accepted on your behalf, because they are deformed and flawed.’”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “When an ox or sheep or goat is born, it shall remain seven days with its mother, and from the eighth day on it shall be acceptable as a food offering to the LORD.
verses.kjv: When a bullock, or a sheep, or a goat, is brought forth, then it shall be seven days under the dam; and from the eighth day and thenceforth it shall be accepted for an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: “When an ox, a sheep, or a goat is born, it must remain with its mother for seven days. From the eighth day on, it will be acceptable as an offering made by fire to the LORD.

verses.esv: But you shall not kill an ox or a sheep and her young in one day.
verses.kjv: And whether it be cow or ewe, ye shall not kill it and her young both in one day.
verses.bsb: But you must not slaughter an ox or a sheep on the same day as its young.

verses.esv: And when you sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving to the LORD, you shall sacrifice it so that you may be accepted.
verses.kjv: And when ye will offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving unto the Lord, offer it at your own will.
verses.bsb: When you sacrifice a thank offering to the LORD, offer it so that it may be acceptable on your behalf.

verses.esv: It shall be eaten on the same day; you shall leave none of it until morning: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: On the same day it shall be eaten up; ye shall leave none of it until the morrow: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: It must be eaten that same day. Do not leave any of it until morning. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: “So you shall keep my commandments and do them: I am the LORD.
verses.kjv: Therefore shall ye keep my commandments, and do them: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: You are to keep My commandments and practice them. I am the LORD.

verses.esv: And you shall not profane my holy name, that I may be sanctified among the people of Israel. I am the LORD who sanctifies you,
verses.kjv: Neither shall ye profane my holy name; but I will be hallowed among the children of Israel: I am the Lord which hallow you,
verses.bsb: You must not profane My holy name. I must be acknowledged as holy among the Israelites. I am the LORD who sanctifies you,

verses.esv: who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I am the LORD.”
verses.kjv: That brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I am the Lord.
verses.bsb: who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the LORD.”

verses.esv: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to the people of Israel and say to them, These are the appointed feasts of the LORD that you shall proclaim as holy convocations; they are my appointed feasts.
verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, Concerning the feasts of the Lord, which ye shall proclaim to be holy convocations, even these are my feasts.
verses.bsb: “Speak to the Israelites and say to them, ‘These are My appointed feasts, the feasts of the LORD that you are to proclaim as sacred assemblies.

verses.esv: “Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day is a Sabbath of solemn rest, a holy convocation. You shall do no work. It is a Sabbath to the LORD in all your dwelling places.
verses.kjv: Six days shall work be done: but the seventh day is the sabbath of rest, an holy convocation; ye shall do no work therein: it is the sabbath of the Lord in all your dwellings.
verses.bsb: For six days work may be done, but the seventh day is a Sabbath of complete rest, a day of sacred assembly. You must not do any work; wherever you live, it is a Sabbath to the LORD.

verses.esv: “These are the appointed feasts of the LORD, the holy convocations, which you shall proclaim at the time appointed for them.
verses.kjv: ¶ These are the feasts of the Lord, even holy convocations, which ye shall proclaim in their seasons.
verses.bsb: These are the LORD’s appointed feasts, the sacred assemblies you are to proclaim at their appointed times.

verses.esv: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at twilight, is the LORD's Passover.
verses.kjv: In the fourteenth day of the first month at even is the Lord’s passover.
verses.bsb: The Passover to the LORD begins at twilight on the fourteenth day of the first month.

verses.esv: And on the fifteenth day of the same month is the Feast of Unleavened Bread to the LORD; for seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
verses.kjv: And on the fifteenth day of the same month is the feast of unleavened bread unto the Lord: seven days ye must eat unleavened bread.
verses.bsb: On the fifteenth day of the same month begins the Feast of Unleavened Bread to the LORD. For seven days you must eat unleavened bread.

verses.esv: On the first day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not do any ordinary work.
verses.kjv: In the first day ye shall have an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.
verses.bsb: On the first day you are to hold a sacred assembly; you are not to do any regular work.

verses.esv: But you shall present a food offering to the LORD for seven days. On the seventh day is a holy convocation; you shall not do any ordinary work.”
verses.kjv: But ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the Lord seven days: in the seventh day is an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.
verses.bsb: For seven days you are to present an offering made by fire to the LORD. On the seventh day there shall be a sacred assembly; you must not do any regular work.’”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to the people of Israel and say to them, When you come into the land that I give you and reap its harvest, you shall bring the sheaf of the firstfruits of your harvest to the priest,
verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye be come into the land which I give unto you, and shall reap the harvest thereof, then ye shall bring a sheaf of the firstfruits of your harvest unto the priest:
verses.bsb: “Speak to the Israelites and say, ‘When you enter the land that I am giving you and you reap its harvest, you are to bring to the priest a sheaf of the firstfruits of your harvest.

verses.esv: and he shall wave the sheaf before the LORD, so that you may be accepted. On the day after the Sabbath the priest shall wave it.
verses.kjv: And he shall wave the sheaf before the Lord, to be accepted for you: on the morrow after the sabbath the priest shall wave it.
verses.bsb: And he shall wave the sheaf before the LORD so that it may be accepted on your behalf; the priest is to wave it on the day after the Sabbath.

verses.esv: And on the day when you wave the sheaf, you shall offer a male lamb a year old without blemish as a burnt offering to the LORD.
verses.kjv: And ye shall offer that day when ye wave the sheaf an he lamb without blemish of the first year for a burnt offering unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: On the day you wave the sheaf, you shall offer a year-old lamb without blemish as a burnt offering to the LORD,

verses.esv: And the grain offering with it shall be two tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil, a food offering to the LORD with a pleasing aroma, and the drink offering with it shall be of wine, a fourth of a hin.
verses.kjv: And the meat offering thereof shall be two tenth deals of fine flour mingled with oil, an offering made by fire unto the Lord for a sweet savour: and the drink offering thereof shall be of wine, the fourth part of an hin.
verses.bsb: along with its grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil—an offering made by fire to the LORD, a pleasing aroma—and its drink offering of a quarter hin of wine.

verses.esv: And you shall eat neither bread nor grain parched or fresh until this same day, until you have brought the offering of your God: it is a statute forever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
verses.kjv: And ye shall eat neither bread, nor parched corn, nor green ears, until the selfsame day that ye have brought an offering unto your God: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
verses.bsb: You must not eat any bread or roasted or new grain until the very day you have brought this offering to your God. This is to be a permanent statute for the generations to come, wherever you live.

verses.esv: “You shall count seven full weeks from the day after the Sabbath, from the day that you brought the sheaf of the wave offering.
verses.kjv: ¶ And ye shall count unto you from the morrow after the sabbath, from the day that ye brought the sheaf of the wave offering; seven sabbaths shall be complete:
verses.bsb: From the day after the Sabbath, the day you brought the sheaf of the wave offering, you are to count off seven full weeks.

verses.esv: You shall count fifty days to the day after the seventh Sabbath. Then you shall present a grain offering of new grain to the LORD.
verses.kjv: Even unto the morrow after the seventh sabbath shall ye number fifty days; and ye shall offer a new meat offering unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: You shall count off fifty days until the day after the seventh Sabbath, and then present an offering of new grain to the LORD.

verses.esv: You shall bring from your dwelling places two loaves of bread to be waved, made of two tenths of an ephah. They shall be of fine flour, and they shall be baked with leaven, as firstfruits to the LORD.
verses.kjv: Ye shall bring out of your habitations two wave loaves of two tenth deals: they shall be of fine flour; they shall be baken with leaven; they are the firstfruits unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Bring two loaves of bread from your dwellings as a wave offering, each made from two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour, baked with leaven, as the firstfruits to the LORD.

verses.esv: And you shall present with the bread seven lambs a year old without blemish, and one bull from the herd and two rams. They shall be a burnt offering to the LORD, with their grain offering and their drink offerings, a food offering with a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
verses.kjv: And ye shall offer with the bread seven lambs without blemish of the first year, and one young bullock, and two rams: they shall be for a burnt offering unto the Lord, with their meat offering, and their drink offerings, even an offering made by fire, of sweet savour unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Along with the bread you are to present seven unblemished male lambs a year old, one young bull, and two rams. They will be a burnt offering to the LORD, together with their grain offerings and drink offerings—an offering made by fire, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.

verses.esv: And you shall offer one male goat for a sin offering, and two male lambs a year old as a sacrifice of peace offerings.
verses.kjv: Then ye shall sacrifice one kid of the goats for a sin offering, and two lambs of the first year for a sacrifice of peace offerings.
verses.bsb: You shall also prepare one male goat as a sin offering and two male lambs a year old as a peace offering.

verses.esv: And the priest shall wave them with the bread of the firstfruits as a wave offering before the LORD, with the two lambs. They shall be holy to the LORD for the priest.
verses.kjv: And the priest shall wave them with the bread of the firstfruits for a wave offering before the Lord, with the two lambs: they shall be holy to the Lord for the priest.
verses.bsb: The priest is to wave the lambs as a wave offering before the LORD, together with the bread of the firstfruits. The bread and the two lambs shall be holy to the LORD for the priest.

verses.esv: And you shall make a proclamation on the same day. You shall hold a holy convocation. You shall not do any ordinary work. It is a statute forever in all your dwelling places throughout your generations.
verses.kjv: And ye shall proclaim on the selfsame day, that it may be an holy convocation unto you: ye shall do no servile work therein: it shall be a statute for ever in all your dwellings throughout your generations.
verses.bsb: On that same day you are to proclaim a sacred assembly, and you must not do any regular work. This is to be a permanent statute wherever you live for the generations to come.

verses.esv: “And when you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap your field right up to its edge, nor shall you gather the gleanings after your harvest. You shall leave them for the poor and for the sojourner: I am the LORD your God.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not make clean riddance of the corners of thy field when thou reapest, neither shalt thou gather any gleaning of thy harvest: thou shalt leave them unto the poor, and to the stranger: I am the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: When you reap the harvest of your land, do not reap all the way to the edges of your field or gather the gleanings of your harvest. Leave them for the poor and the foreign resident. I am the LORD your God.’”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: The LORD also said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to the people of Israel, saying, In the seventh month, on the first day of the month, you shall observe a day of solemn rest, a memorial proclaimed with blast of trumpets, a holy convocation.
verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, In the seventh month, in the first day of the month, shall ye have a sabbath, a memorial of blowing of trumpets, an holy convocation.
verses.bsb: “Speak to the Israelites and say, ‘On the first day of the seventh month you are to have a day of rest, a sacred assembly announced by trumpet blasts.

verses.esv: You shall not do any ordinary work, and you shall present a food offering to the LORD.”
verses.kjv: Ye shall do no servile work therein: but ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: You must not do any regular work, but you are to present an offering made by fire to the LORD.’”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Again the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Now on the tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement. It shall be for you a time of holy convocation, and you shall afflict yourselves and present a food offering to the LORD.
verses.kjv: Also on the tenth day of this seventh month there shall be a day of atonement: it shall be an holy convocation unto you; and ye shall afflict your souls, and offer an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: “The tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement. You shall hold a sacred assembly and humble yourselves, and present an offering made by fire to the LORD.

verses.esv: And you shall not do any work on that very day, for it is a Day of Atonement, to make atonement for you before the LORD your God.
verses.kjv: And ye shall do no work in that same day: for it is a day of atonement, to make an atonement for you before the Lord your God.
verses.bsb: On this day you are not to do any work, for it is the Day of Atonement, when atonement is made for you before the LORD your God.

verses.esv: For whoever is not afflicted on that very day shall be cut off from his people.
verses.kjv: For whatsoever soul it be that shall not be afflicted in that same day, he shall be cut off from among his people.
verses.bsb: If anyone does not humble himself on this day, he must be cut off from his people.

verses.esv: And whoever does any work on that very day, that person I will destroy from among his people.
verses.kjv: And whatsoever soul it be that doeth any work in that same day, the same soul will I destroy from among his people.
verses.bsb: I will destroy from among his people anyone who does any work on this day.

verses.esv: You shall not do any work. It is a statute forever throughout your generations in all your dwelling places.
verses.kjv: Ye shall do no manner of work: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
verses.bsb: You are not to do any work at all. This is a permanent statute for the generations to come, wherever you live.

verses.esv: It shall be to you a Sabbath of solemn rest, and you shall afflict yourselves. On the ninth day of the month beginning at evening, from evening to evening shall you keep your Sabbath.”
verses.kjv: It shall be unto you a sabbath of rest, and ye shall afflict your souls: in the ninth day of the month at even, from even unto even, shall ye celebrate your sabbath.
verses.bsb: It will be a Sabbath of complete rest for you, and you shall humble yourselves. From the evening of the ninth day of the month until the following evening you are to keep your Sabbath.”

verses.esv: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses,

verses.esv: “Speak to the people of Israel, saying, On the fifteenth day of this seventh month and for seven days is the Feast of Booths to the LORD.
verses.kjv: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, The fifteenth day of this seventh month shall be the feast of tabernacles for seven days unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: “Speak to the Israelites and say, ‘On the fifteenth day of the seventh month the Feast of Tabernacles to the LORD begins, and it continues for seven days.

verses.esv: On the first day shall be a holy convocation; you shall not do any ordinary work.
verses.kjv: On the first day shall be an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.
verses.bsb: On the first day there shall be a sacred assembly. You must not do any regular work.
